reason: Response A demonstrates the more sophisticated emotional intelligence and self-aware restraint characteristic of genuine Claude outputs, notably the meta-awareness of not burdening the recipient with managing the apologizer's guilt and refusing to demand forgiveness on its own timeline. Response B is more generic and formulaic, leaning on stock apology tropes ("impact matters more than intention," "the chance to apologize," "how we grow closer") that read like an imitation aiming at surface-level Claude vocabulary. A's concrete, anti-performative framing and structural confidence point to it being the authentic Haiku response.
